name: Dependabot.yml Vocabulary
description: Controlled vocabulary for the GitHub dependabot.yml configuration domain.

terms.definition: A YAML document at .github/dependabot.yml that instructs GitHub Dependabot how to monitor and update dependencies.
A supported dependency manager (npm, pip, maven, cargo, github-actions, docker, etc.) that Dependabot monitors.
A single configuration block in the updates array, scoped to one ecosystem and directory.
Cadence configuration controlling how frequently Dependabot checks for updates.
Frequency value (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semiannually, yearly, or cron) for an update check.
Named bundle of dependencies that should be updated together in a single pull request.
Delay window between when a dependency version is published and when Dependabot proposes adopting it.
Filter that explicitly opts a dependency or dependency-type into update consideration.
Filter that excludes a dependency, version range, or update-type from updates.
Authenticated upstream package source referenced by an update entry.
Policy controlling how Dependabot rewrites manifest version constraints (auto, increase, increase-if-necessary, lockfile-only, widen).
Policy controlling whether Dependabot automatically rebases its open pull requests.
Cap on the number of concurrent open Dependabot pull requests for an update entry.
Branch other than the repository default that Dependabot opens pull requests against.
Dependabot pull request that resolves a known vulnerability advisory.
Dependabot pull request that adopts a newer non-vulnerable release.
